perturbation.original_text: Pursuant and subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Joint Filing Agreement is attached, and any amendments thereto may be filed without the necessity of filing additional joint filing agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Joint Filing Agreement is attached, and any amendments thereto may be filed only with the necessity of filing additional joint filing agreements for each amendment.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the term 'Pursuant' and changing 'without the necessity' to 'only with the necessity,' a contradiction is created regarding the filing of amendments. The original clause states that amendments can be filed without needing new joint filing agreements, while the modified version requires a new agreement for each amendment. This significantly alters the obligations of the parties and increases administrative burden. The insertion of 'only with' directly contradicts the initial intent of allowing amendments without further agreements, leading to enforcement uncertainty regarding how amendments must be filed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Joint Filing Agreement is attached, and any amendments thereto may be filed only with the necessity of filing additional joint filing agreements for each amendment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent by requiring new joint filing agreements for each amendment, while the original text stated amendments could be filed without this requirement. This creates a direct conflict within the document and increases the administrative burden, triggering the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.
